seo.title: E-Commerce AI Coding Assistant AI Agent Checklist
seo.description: Production readiness checklist for ai coding assistant AI agents in e-commerce. Covers FTC advertising guidelines, CCPA/GDPR, PCI DSS for payments.

content.intro: Deploying an AI coding assistant in an e-commerce environment introduces unique risks where generated code directly impacts payment flows, product listings, pricing logic, and customer data handling. A single misconfigured pricing function or insecure checkout snippet can trigger PCI DSS violations, FTC enforcement actions, or mass customer refund disputes. This checklist ensures your AI coding agent produces code that is not only functionally correct but compliant with the regulatory and business requirements governing e-commerce operations.

sections.heading: Code Correctness & E-Commerce Logic Validation
sections.description: Ensure AI-generated code accurately implements business-critical e-commerce logic including pricing, inventory, and checkout flows before any code reaches production.

items.label: Pricing Calculation Unit Tests
items.description: Require the AI agent to generate and pass unit tests for every pricing function it produces, including edge cases for discounts, tax calculations, and currency conversions. Validate outputs against known-correct values before merging.

items.label: Checkout Flow Integration Testing
items.description: Run AI-generated checkout and cart code through end-to-end integration tests in a sandbox environment that mirrors production payment gateway configurations, verifying totals, tax, and shipping costs are calculated correctly.

items.label: Inventory Decrement Logic Verification
items.description: Validate that any AI-generated inventory management code correctly handles concurrent requests to prevent overselling, using race condition tests and database transaction rollback scenarios.

items.label: Return & Refund Policy Code Alignment
items.description: Cross-reference AI-generated refund processing logic against your published return policy documentation to ensure refund eligibility windows, restocking fees, and partial refund rules are faithfully implemented.

items.label: Promotional Code & Discount Boundary Testing
items.description: Test AI-generated coupon and promotional logic for stacking vulnerabilities, negative price exploits, and expiration date enforcement to prevent revenue loss from discount abuse.

items.label: Product Attribute Rendering Accuracy
items.description: Verify that AI-generated product display code accurately renders all attributes (size, color, SKU, price) from the data source without truncation, rounding errors, or mismatched field mappings.

items.label: Sandbox Execution Before Repository Merge
items.description: Enforce a policy that all AI-generated code must execute successfully in an isolated sandbox environment with representative e-commerce data fixtures before any pull request is approved.

sections.heading: Security, Credentials & PCI DSS Compliance
sections.description: Prevent the AI coding agent from introducing security vulnerabilities or leaking sensitive credentials into e-commerce codebases that handle payment card data and customer PII.

items.label: Automated Credential Leak Scanning
items.description: Integrate a secrets detection tool (e.g., GitGuardian, TruffleHog) into the AI agent's output pipeline to automatically reject any generated code containing hardcoded API keys, payment gateway tokens, database passwords, or OAuth secrets.

items.label: PCI DSS Scope Isolation Check
items.description: Review all AI-generated payment-related code to confirm it does not log, store, or transmit raw card numbers, CVVs, or full PANs. Verify tokenization and iframe/redirect patterns are used instead of direct card data handling.

items.label: OWASP Top 10 Vulnerability Scan
items.description: Run AI-generated code through a static analysis security testing (SAST) tool configured with e-commerce-specific rules to detect SQL injection in product search, XSS in review forms, and CSRF in checkout endpoints.

items.label: Dependency Safety & CVE Screening
items.description: Automatically scan any new npm, pip, or composer packages introduced by the AI agent against the CVE database and your approved dependency allowlist before installation in the e-commerce codebase.

items.label: Insecure Direct Object Reference (IDOR) Review
items.description: Audit AI-generated order management and account endpoints for IDOR vulnerabilities that could allow customers to access other users' order history, addresses, or payment methods.

items.label: Environment Variable Usage Enforcement
items.description: Validate that AI-generated code references payment processor credentials, shipping API keys, and third-party service tokens exclusively through environment variables, never inline in source files.

items.label: Rate Limiting on AI-Generated Auth Endpoints
items.description: Confirm that any login, password reset, or account creation code generated by the AI agent includes rate limiting and account lockout logic to prevent credential stuffing attacks on customer accounts.

items.label: TLS/HTTPS Enforcement in Generated Network Code
items.description: Reject any AI-generated code that initiates HTTP (non-TLS) connections to payment processors, shipping providers, or customer data APIs, enforcing HTTPS-only communication patterns.

sections.heading: CCPA/GDPR & Customer Data Handling
sections.description: Ensure AI-generated code respects customer privacy rights and complies with CCPA and GDPR data handling requirements across all e-commerce data flows.

items.label: PII Minimization in Generated Data Models
items.description: Review AI-generated database schemas and API payloads to confirm only necessary customer fields are collected and stored. Flag any generated code that captures additional PII (e.g., device fingerprints, behavioral data) without explicit consent logic.

items.label: Data Deletion Endpoint Completeness
items.description: When the AI agent generates customer account deletion features, verify the generated code removes or anonymizes PII from all tables, caches, logs, and third-party sync queues, not just the primary user record.

items.label: Consent Flag Propagation in Generated Code
items.description: Audit AI-generated marketing and analytics integration code to confirm it checks and respects customer consent flags before firing tracking pixels, email subscriptions, or behavioral analytics events.

items.label: Data Export (Portability) Function Validation
items.description: Test AI-generated data export features to ensure they return a complete, accurate, and machine-readable record of all customer data held, satisfying GDPR Article 20 and CCPA data portability requirements.

items.label: Third-Party Data Sharing Audit in Generated Integrations
items.description: Review any AI-generated third-party integration code (shipping, analytics, CRM) to identify what customer data is transmitted and confirm it aligns with your privacy policy disclosures and data processing agreements.

items.label: Cookie Consent Gate in Frontend Code
items.description: Verify that AI-generated frontend JavaScript does not initialize analytics, advertising, or personalization scripts before the customer's cookie consent status is resolved and affirmative consent is confirmed.

items.label: Log Sanitization for PII
items.description: Scan AI-generated logging and error handling code to ensure customer email addresses, phone numbers, and order details are masked or excluded from application logs and error reporting services.

sections.heading: FTC Compliance, ADA Accessibility & License Governance
sections.description: Validate that AI-generated code supports accurate product claims, accessible user interfaces, and uses only license-compatible open source dependencies in the e-commerce platform.

items.label: Dynamic Pricing Display Accuracy Audit
items.description: Test AI-generated price rendering code across all customer touchpoints (product page, cart, email, receipt) to confirm prices are consistent and match the authoritative pricing database, preventing FTC deceptive pricing violations.

items.label: Sale & Discount Label Substantiation Check
items.description: Review AI-generated promotional badge and sale label logic to ensure 'was' prices, percentage discounts, and 'limited time' claims are derived from verifiable historical pricing data, not hardcoded or fabricated values.

items.label: ADA/WCAG 2.1 AA Accessibility Linting
items.description: Run AI-generated frontend components through an automated accessibility linter (e.g., axe-core, Lighthouse) to catch missing alt text on product images, insufficient color contrast, and keyboard navigation gaps that violate ADA requirements.

items.label: Screen Reader Compatibility for Checkout Flow
items.description: Manually or programmatically test AI-generated checkout form code with a screen reader to verify all input labels, error messages, and order confirmation states are properly announced and navigable.

items.label: Open Source License Compatibility Scan
items.description: Automatically scan all dependencies introduced by the AI agent using a license compliance tool (e.g., FOSSA, Snyk License) to flag GPL, AGPL, or other copyleft licenses that may conflict with your commercial e-commerce platform's licensing.

items.label: AI-Generated Code Attribution & IP Review
items.description: Establish a policy for reviewing AI-generated code blocks that closely replicate known open source implementations, ensuring your organization is not inadvertently incorporating unlicensed third-party code into the commercial codebase.

items.label: Return Policy Logic Fidelity Test
items.description: Create automated tests that validate AI-generated return eligibility and refund calculation code against every condition in your published return policy, including exceptions for final sale items, digital goods, and international orders.

items.label: Advertising Claim Rendering Validation
items.description: Audit AI-generated product description and feature highlight rendering code to ensure it does not modify, truncate, or embellish supplier-provided product claims in ways that could constitute unsubstantiated advertising under FTC guidelines.

regulatoryNotes.heading: Regulatory Considerations
notes: FTC Deceptive Pricing Rule: AI-generated pricing display code must reflect genuine former prices when showing strikethrough or 'was' pricing. The FTC requires that reference prices reflect actual prices at which the product was offered for a reasonably substantial period. Automated tests should verify that AI-generated promotional logic pulls reference prices from auditable historical pricing records, not static values.

PCI DSS v4.0 Requirement 6 (Secure Systems Development): Any AI coding agent contributing to payment-related code is subject to PCI DSS Requirement 6, which mandates secure development practices, code review, and vulnerability management. AI-generated code touching the cardholder data environment must undergo the same security review process as human-written code, including SAST scanning and peer review before deployment.

CCPA/GDPR Data Minimization & Purpose Limitation: AI-generated data collection and storage code must adhere to the principle of data minimization—collecting only what is necessary for the stated purpose. Generated code that introduces new data fields or tracking mechanisms must be reviewed against your privacy policy and, where required, trigger a Data Protection Impact Assessment (DPIA) before deployment.

ADA Title III & Web Accessibility: U.S. federal courts have consistently held that e-commerce websites are places of public accommodation under ADA Title III. AI-generated frontend code must meet WCAG 2.1 Level AA standards. Failure to remediate accessibility barriers in AI-generated checkout, product search, or account management interfaces exposes the business to demand letters and class action litigation, which have resulted in settlements exceeding $100,000 for major retailers.

cta.heading: Automate Your E-Commerce Agent Compliance
cta.description: Use Vex to continuously monitor your AI coding agent's outputs against PCI DSS, FTC, CCPA, and ADA requirements—catching compliance violations before they reach production and protecting your revenue, customers, and brand.
